message:
chore(beads): record request-scope API drift and FsGeometry field issues

Add bd-raks (closed: fix missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests)
and bd-yhro (open: fix request-scope API drift in ffs-core) issue
records to track the RequestScope migration work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-qexf","title":"ffs-cli ext4 group info loads full image unnecessarily","description":"build_ext4_group_info uses std::fs::read + Ext4ImageReader even though it only needs superblock-derived group descriptor windows. Replace with bounded descriptor reads via read_file_region and parse Ext4GroupDesc directly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:45:50.280116792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:56:57.846860907Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:56:57.846831262Z","close_reason":"Completed: build_ext4_group_info now reads/parses only ext4 group descriptor windows via read_file_region + Ext4GroupDesc::parse_from_bytes, removing full-image std::fs::read dependency. Added regression test build_ext4_group_info_reads_descriptors_from_disk_regions; ran fmt, targeted tests, check, clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","memory","reliability"]}
 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-raks","title":"Fix missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation by updating stale `FsGeometry` initializers in `ffs-inode` to include the required `desc_size` and `feature_ro_compat` fields.

## Scope

1. Reproduce the current `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` failure via `rch`.
2. Patch the failing `FsGeometry` initializers in `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs`.
3. Re-run targeted `rch` checks for `ffs-inode` and, if clean, the workspace check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs` compiles with current `FsGeometry` definition
- Targeted `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets` passes
- Workspace check moves past this failure or reports the next unrelated blocker

 {"id":"bd-rale","title":"Improve FfsError::Io errno mapping with ErrorKind fallback","description":"When std::io::Error has no raw_os_error() (e.g. created via Error::new()), to_errno() falls back to EIO. Improve by mapping io::ErrorKind to appropriate errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, etc.) before falling back to EIO. This gives FUSE callers more specific error codes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T00:49:02.204994325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073573327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073546988Z","close_reason":"Improved to_errno() Io variant: now maps ErrorKind to specific errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, AlreadyExists→EEXIST, WouldBlock→EAGAIN, InvalidInput/InvalidData→EINVAL, TimedOut→ETIMEDOUT, Interrupted→EINTR) when raw_os_error() is None, instead of always falling back to EIO. Added test io_error_kind_mapping_without_raw_errno covering all mapped kinds. Also fixed two pre-existing clippy pedantic lints. All 20 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sik","title":"ffs-types: Checked arithmetic + alignment helpers (block math, offsets)","description":"Goal: centralize all tricky integer math used by on-disk parsing and block mapping.

Deliverables:
- checked_add_bytes(offset, len) -> ByteOffset end
- checked_mul_block(block_nr, block_size) -> ByteOffset
- align_down/align_up helpers with overflow checking
- conversions with explicit error paths (no silent truncation)

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover overflow/edge cases.
- Call sites in ffs-block/ffs-ondisk/ffs-core prefer these helpers over ad-hoc math.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:24.565357285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T08:41:43.432870696Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T08:41:43.432852562Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}

 {"id":"bd-xzn0","title":"Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)","description":"# Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)

## GOAL
Replace traditional free-space bitmaps with succinct data structures (rank/select bitmaps) that support O(1) rank and select queries while using near-optimal space.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 7.1: Succinct bitmaps (Jacobson 1989, Clark 1996) store bitmaps in n + o(n) bits while supporting:
- rank(i): count set bits in positions [0, i) — O(1)
- select(k): find position of k-th set bit — O(1)

For free-space bitmaps, this enables:
- O(1) "how many free blocks in range?" (rank)
- O(1) "find k-th free block" (select) — replaces linear scan for allocation

## DESIGN
- Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) + blocks (every 2^9 bits)
- Select via rank binary search or Vigna's select structure
- Space overhead: ~6.25% over raw bitmap (vs 0% for plain bitmap, but queries are O(n))
- Integration: replace ffs-ext4 block bitmap and inode bitmap

## APPLICABILITY
- Optimization for large filesystems where bitmap scans are expensive
- Most impactful when block groups are large or filesystem is >90% full
- Could also accelerate mballoc buddy allocator queries

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select
- [ ] Space overhead < 10% over raw bitmap
- [ ] Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for block/inode bitmaps","acceptance_criteria":"Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select operations. Space overhead below 10% over raw bitmap. Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) plus blocks (every 2^9 bits). Drop-in replacement for block and inode bitmaps via BlockBitmap trait. Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap at 50%, 80%, 95% fullness. Benchmark: rank/select latency vs linear scan. Unit tests: rank correctness for all-zeros, all-ones, random patterns. Unit tests: select correctness for sparse and dense bitmaps. Unit tests: round-trip serialization/deserialization. Property test: rank(select(k)) == k for all valid k.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:45.958783809Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:30:55.736127822Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:30:55.736029688Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["datastructure","freespace","succinct"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xzn0","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.714682331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-xzn0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Include targeted tests and gate verification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:15:46.869645348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:31:17.043630327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:31:17.043610981Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ye4","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement extent mapping (ffs-extent) for regular files","description":"Goal: map file logical offsets to physical blocks using the ext4 extent tree.

Deliverables:
- ffs-extent: a function that takes an inode extent root and returns an iterator of (logical_block -> physical_start,len,unwritten?).
- Support depth>0 extent trees by reading extent blocks from disk (requires BlockDevice).
- Provide a read_extent_at(file_block) helper for file reads.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover: leaf-only extents and index+leaf extents.
- Verified on a fixture file with known extents (compare to debugfs stats or golden outputs).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:46.487585491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:36:21.615868955Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:36:21.615851281Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ye4","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.350893907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ye4","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.455657288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-yhro","title":"Fix request-scope API drift in ffs-core","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation after the request-scope API changes in `ffs-core`/`vfs` drifted out of sync.

## Observed failures
- `commit_request_scope` added with invalid visibility / wrong trait placement
- `TxnAbortReason` path no longer resolves
- `fsyncdir` implementation signature no longer matches trait
- `FfsError::Conflict` variant no longer exists

## Acceptance criteria
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` moves past the current `ffs-core` request-scope failures
- `ffs-core` trait and impl signatures are consistent
- error mapping uses a valid `FfsError` variant or helper

All gates pass (check, clippy, fmt).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-z7n","title":"Track: Foundations (ffs-types + ffs-error)","description":"Define canonical newtypes, parsing primitives, and error model that every other crate depends on.\n\nAcceptance: all normative types (BlockNumber, BlockSize, InodeNumber strategy, TxnId/CommitSeq/Snapshot, ByteOffset, DeviceId, etc.) exist in ffs-types with documented invariants; ffs-error has stable variant set + errno mapping used by FUSE. All other crates use these types (no local duplicates).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:25.718123153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:01:32.112602115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:01:32.112583771Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.228334190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-1ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.370722205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-2oa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.603178334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-3hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.526401085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-owq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.446053435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-sik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.299955368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-z7n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Why this track is first-class: filesystem bugs love unit confusion (bytes vs blocks) and inconsistent error semantics (silent truncation, wrong errno).

North star:
- ffs-types: strong newtypes + checked arithmetic so invariants are enforced at compile time.
- ffs-error: a stable user-facing error set + errno mapping for FUSE.

If this track is sloppy, every other track becomes untestable and unsafe.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:38Z"}]}
